creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
I see all segments when tracing e.g. /bin/sh

# procstat -v $$
  PID              START                END PRT  RES PRES REF SHD FLAG  TP PATH
12717     0x10a3fd763000     0x10a3fd76c000 r--    7   39  36  18 CN--- vn /bin/sh
12717     0x10a3fd76c000     0x10a3fd78c000 r-x   32   39  36  18 CN--- vn /bin/sh
12717     0x10a3fd78c000     0x10a3fd78d000 r--    1    0   1   0 CN--- vn /bin/sh
12717     0x10a3fd78d000     0x10a3fd78e000 rw-    1    0   1   0 CN--- vn /bin/sh
...

How do I get memcheck-amd64-freebsd to run but stop before doing anything?

$ /usr/local/libexec/valgrind/memcheck-amd64-freebsd
valgrind: You cannot run '/usr/local/libexec/valgrind/memcheck-amd64-freebsd' directly.
valgrind: You should use $prefix/bin/valgrind.
